evals.prompt: I have four findings from a study on enterprise AI adoption and need a board-ready executive summary to support a $5M AI transformation budget decision for Q1. Findings: (1) Enterprise AI adoption reached 72% of Fortune 500 firms in 2024, up from 41% in 2022 (Gartner). (2) Implementation costs average $2.3M for mid-market deployments with a 14-month median payback (McKinsey). (3) LLM-based automation reduces analyst hours by 40% in back-office workflows across 3 controlled pilots (HBR). (4) Data governance gaps blocked deployment in 31% of attempted rollouts (Forrester). Write the executive summary.
evals.expected_output: A 1-2 page executive summary with BLUF, Key Findings, Recommendation, and Risks & Caveats sections, at least one quantified figure carried through with a citation, a numbered footnote list resolving to the four sources, and no method narration or alternatives discussion.
expectations: The BLUF section appears first, before any background, and states the conclusion and recommended action in one paragraph, with a heading that literally contains BLUF
Key Findings has 3-5 bullets, each a load-bearing fact with its business consequence and a citation marker
The Recommendation is one specific, actionable directive addressing the $5M budget decision with What/Why/How/Risk
The document stays within a 1-2 page ceiling with no methodology narration or alternatives-considered discussion

evals.prompt: Review this executive-summary draft: it opens with a Background section on our data warehouse review, followed by a Methodology section describing how cost data was collected, then a vague Findings section with no numbers, and ends with a 'Next Steps' heading that says we should keep evaluating options. What's wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the missing BLUF as the core defect — the summary leads with background and method instead of the conclusion and recommended action — and flags the vague, unquantified findings and the buried, non-actionable recommendation.
expectations: Names the missing BLUF section as the central flaw, not a stylistic nitpick, and explains it must open the document before any background
Flags that the Methodology narration violates the genre's rule against exposing intermediate analysis or method
Flags that the findings lack a quantified figure and citation, and that 'keep evaluating options' is not a specific, actionable recommendation
Notes the missing Risks & Caveats section and footnote list, meaning the draft cannot stand alone

evals.prompt: We just finished a full market-research study on entering the Southeast Asian logistics market, with sampling methodology, fieldwork disclosure, and a technical appendix. Should the whole thing be written as an exec-summary?
evals.expected_output: Recommends market-research-report for the full study and explains that exec-summary only produces the leadership-summary section that introduces it — the sampling basis, fieldwork, and technical appendix belong in the fuller report, not the 1-2 page BLUF brief.
expectations: Recommends market-research-report (or notes the ESOMAR composable mode) for the full study rather than writing the whole thing as exec-summary
Explains exec-summary's defining trait is a short, standalone BLUF brief for decision-makers, not a vehicle for sampling methodology or a technical appendix
Distinguishes this from prd (requirements) and clarifies that composable mode lets exec-summary render only the management-summary section that introduces the fuller report

evals.prompt: Produce a PTES-style penetration-test leadership summary by composing exec-summary into PTES mode. Findings: an unauthenticated admin panel was exposed on the external attack surface; the password policy permits credential stuffing at scale; network segmentation limits lateral movement from the DMZ. Render it expanded with the PTES Posture, Risk Profile, and Roadmap sub-elements in addition to BLUF and Key Findings.
evals.expected_output: A PTES leadership summary with BLUF and Key Findings plus the additive PTES sub-elements Posture, Risk Profile, and Roadmap, still capped at 1-2 pages and free of any internal finding-ID leakage.
expectations: BLUF and Key Findings are still present and unchanged in shape, since PTES sub-elements are additive, not a replacement for the standard structure
Includes a distinct security Posture statement, a business Risk Profile derived from the findings, and a prioritized remediation Roadmap
Does not claim ESOMAR-format conformance or introduce ESOMAR-specific caveats, since PTES mode — not ESOMAR mode — was requested
